As for why it's so insanely low, he's an easy upper bound for the probability: There are a total of 800*608 = 486400 pixels on the screen. For symmetry reasons we may assume the first half of pixels are white, the second half are black. It's a 50% chance for the first pixel you position to be white. Then for the next pixel, you have fewer white pixels left than black ones, so the chance is less than 50% that the second pixel is placed correctly.

Until all white pixels are placed, 1/2 is always an upper bound of the probability that you next place the white pixel correctly, so the probability is no greater than (1/2)^243200, since that's how many white pixels there are. In reality the probability is much lower.
